_readme: Verified official broadcast-channel registry (Roadmap Phase C1).
Discovery prefers KNOWN official channels and their upload playlists over
broad YouTube search (C2): videos.list / playlistItems.list cost 1 quota
unit while search.list costs ~100 (C4). Broad search is a scoped fallback.
Each channel scores candidates during broadcast matching (C3): an official
channel adds authority, an unofficial one is rejected by default. Only
high-confidence official candidates auto-link; medium-confidence ones enter
review. `priority` breaks ties when several official feeds exist for one
match (higher wins). `language`/`region` feed preferred-feed selection.
VERIFICATION RULE (mirrors the FACEIT registry pass): `channelId` is null
until the REAL YouTube channel id is confirmed against the live YouTube Data
API, and a null-id channel is kept `enabled: false` so it never drives
discovery on a guess. `officialSourceUrl` is the channel's public handle URL
(a documented fact, not an API id) — the human-checkable source a reviewer
confirms before enabling. `verificationDate` is null until an API check runs.
TO VERIFY (fills channelId + uploadsPlaylistId + verificationDate):
run the `discovery` GitHub Actions workflow with mode=verify-channels,
where the YOUTUBE_API_KEY secret lives. That mode resolves each handle to
its channel id via channels.list (1 unit each), prints the exact channel
name + uploads playlist, and NEVER writes video or compositions. A human
then pastes the confirmed ids here and flips `enabled: true`.
STATUS (2026-07-24): all channels remain UNVERIFIED (channelId null, enabled
false). The YOUTUBE_API_KEY secret is not available in the config/dev
environment, so no channel id could be API-verified here. Per the task rule
— only API-verified official channel ids may be committed — ids stay null
and are NOT guessed. Handles below are the public official sources to verify.
